title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make crispy, authentic Haemul Pajeon, the beloved Korean seafood pancake. This easy recipe brings the flavors of Korea right to your kitchen. Perfect for a delicious meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Perfect Crispy Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A plate of crispy Haemul Pajeon, a traditional Korean seafood pancake, garnished with green onions and served with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy this delicious homemade Haemul Pajeon, a savory Korean seafood pancake that's perfect for sharing!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake featuring a delightful medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions encased in a crispy, golden batter. This traditional dish offers a perfect balance of chewy textures from the seafood and a satisfying crunch from the expertly fried edges. Enjoy it as a hearty snack, appetizer, or a light meal, especially on a rainy day.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid, mussels, oysters, cleaned and chopped into bite-sized pieces
about 8-10 stalks, cut into 3-4 inch lengths

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, minced garlic, and sesame seeds. Stir well and set aside. This dipping sauce will perfectly complement your Haemul Pajeon.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Ensure your seafood mix is thoroughly cleaned and patted dry. Cut larger pieces of seafood, like squid or shrimp, into bite-sized pieces. Wash and trim the green onions, then cut them into 3-4 inch long pieces. Thinly slice the half onion and red chili pepper if using.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch (or potato starch), salt, and black pepper. Gradually add the cold water and the egg, whisking until you have a smooth batter with no lumps. Be careful not to overmix; a few small lumps are fine.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the prepared seafood mix, green onion pieces, and sliced onion to the batter. Gently fold everything together until all ingredients are coated. Try to distribute the seafood and green onions evenly throughout the batter.

instructions.title: Pan-Fry the Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, pour about half of the batter mixture into the skillet, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake. You can arrange additional green onion pieces or chili slices on top for presentation. Cook for 5-7 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish Cooking
instructions.text: Carefully flip the pancake using a wide spatula. Add another tablespoon of oil around the edges if needed. Press down lightly to ensure even cooking. Cook for another 5-7 minutes on the second side until it is also golden brown and the seafood is cooked through. Repeat for the remaining batter.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and slice it into wedges or squares. Serve hot with the prepared dipping sauce on the side. Enjoy your homemade, crispy Korean seafood pancake!

tips: For an extra crispy pancake, ensure your oil is hot before adding the batter and avoid overcrowding the pan. A little cornstarch in the batter also helps achieve that perfect crunch.
Use a variety of fresh seafood for the best flavor and texture. If fresh is unavailable, good quality frozen seafood mix can be used; just make sure to thaw and drain it thoroughly.
Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its optimal crispiness. Pair it with a simple soy-based dipping sauce, perhaps with a touch of gochugaru (Korean chili flakes) for a kick.
article:
Savor the taste of Korea with Haemul Pajeon, a beloved seafood and green onion pancake that captures the essence of Korean comfort food. This delightful dish, often enjoyed on a rainy day or as a shared appetizer, offers a tantalizing combination of crispy edges, a soft interior, and the fresh flavors of the sea. Whether you’re a seasoned Korean food enthusiast or new to its vibrant culinary landscape, learning to make this iconic pancake at home is a rewarding experience that will transport your taste buds straight to Seoul.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?
Haemul Pajeon is a popular Korean savory pancake made primarily from a light batter, abundant green onions (pa), and a generous assortment of fresh seafood (haemul). Unlike some thicker, denser pancakes, a good Haemul Pajeon is characterized by its delicate crispiness on the outside, achieved through careful frying, and a tender, flavorful inside packed with ingredients. Its origins are rooted in traditional Korean home cooking, evolving into a widely cherished dish served in restaurants, street food stalls, and family kitchens across the country. The visual appeal is undeniable, with vibrant green onions and colorful seafood peeking out from the golden-brown batter. It's more than just a pancake; it’s a cultural experience, often paired with makgeolli (Korean rice wine) and enjoyed in good company. The combination of sweet green onions, savory seafood, and a slightly chewy yet crispy texture makes Haemul Pajeon an irresistible treat.

## Ingredients for Haemul Pajeon
The foundation of a great Haemul Pajeon lies in fresh, quality ingredients. Here's what you'll typically need:

For the Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour (or Korean pancake mix for extra crispiness)
*   1/2 cup rice flour (optional, but adds to crispiness)
*   1 1/2 cups cold water (or anchovy broth for deeper flavor)
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of black pepper

For the Seafood:
*   1/2 lb mixed seafood (shrimp, squid rings, mussels, oysters, clams – choose your favorites), cleaned and cut into bite-sized pieces if large
*   Optional: 1-2 small oysters for an authentic touch

For the Vegetables:
*   1 bunch green onions (about 10-12 stalks), cut into 3-4 inch lengths
*   1/2 medium onion, thinly sliced
*   1/2 carrot, julienned (for color and sweetness)
*   1-2 small red or green chilies, thinly sliced (optional, for a kick)

For Frying:
*   Vegetable oil or neutral cooking oil

For the Dipping Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon sesame seeds
*   1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional)
*   1 clove garlic, minced
*   1 tablespoon chopped green onion

## How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step
Making this delightful seafood pancake is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps for a perfect result:

1.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables**: Clean and pat dry all seafood. Cut larger pieces of shrimp or squid into bite-sized portions. Prepare all vegetables as described above.
2.  **Make the Batter**: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour (if using), salt, and pepper. Gradually add the cold water (or broth) while whisking continuously until a smooth, lump-free batter forms. Whisk in the egg until well combined. The consistency should be similar to a thin pancake batter, not too thick.
3.  **Combine Ingredients (Method 1: Mixed)**: For a more integrated pancake, add the prepared seafood, green onions, sliced onion, and julienned carrots directly into the batter and mix gently until everything is coated.
4.  **Combine Ingredients (Method 2: Layered - for visual appeal)**: For a more traditional, visually stunning pancake, heat a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat with a generous amount of oil (about 2-3 tablespoons). Pour a thin layer of batter into the hot pan to form a round base. Immediately arrange the green onion stalks evenly on top, followed by the seafood and other vegetables. Pour a little more batter over the toppings to bind them. This method gives a beautiful presentation with the green onions and seafood visibly embedded.
5.  **Cook the Pancake**: Cook for 5-7 minutes on one side until the edges are golden brown and crispy, and the batter is mostly set. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges.
6.  **Flip and Cook**: Carefully flip the pancake. You might need to use two spatulas if it's large. Cook for another 4-6 minutes on the second side, pressing down gently with a spatula occasionally to ensure even cooking and crispiness. Add more oil if needed.
7.  **Serve**: Once both sides are golden brown and crispy, and the seafood is cooked through, transfer the Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board. Cut into wedges or squares and serve immediately.

## Tips for the Perfect Crispy Haemul Pajeon
Achieving that coveted crispy texture is key to an outstanding Haemul Pajeon. Here are some expert tips:

*   **Cold Water/Broth**: Using very cold water or broth for the batter helps create a crispier texture. Some even suggest adding an ice cube or two to the batter.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter**: Mix just until combined. Overmixing develops gluten, which can make the pancake chewy rather than crispy.
*   **Hot Pan, Plenty of Oil**: Always start with a hot pan and use a generous amount of oil. The oil helps fry the batter rather than just steaming it, leading to a golden, crispy crust. Don't be shy with the oil; you can drain excess later.
*   **Thin Batter Layer**: Don't pour too much batter. A thinner pancake will cook more evenly and be crispier. If you're doing the layered method, make sure the initial batter layer is thin.
*   **Press Down Gently**: After flipping, gently press the pancake with your spatula. This ensures good contact with the hot pan and helps to crisp up the surface.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pan**: Cook one pancake at a time, especially if you're using a standard-sized skillet. Overcrowding lowers the pan temperature and prevents crisping.
*   **Pancake Mix**: If you're new to making Korean pancakes, using a Korean pancake mix (buchim garu) can simplify the process and often yields excellent crispy results due to added starches.

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce
The perfect accompaniment to Haemul Pajeon is a simple yet flavorful dipping sauce. While the pancake itself is packed with flavor, the sauce adds a tangy, savory, and slightly spicy counterpoint that enhances every bite.

**Dipping Sauce Recipe**:
Combine 2 tablespoons soy sauce, 1 tablespoon rice vinegar, 1 teaspoon sesame oil, 1 teaspoon sesame seeds, 1/2 teaspoon gochugaru (optional), 1 minced clove of garlic, and 1 tablespoon chopped green onion in a small bowl. Stir well and let it sit for a few minutes for the flavors to meld.

Serve your hot, crispy Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking. It’s traditionally cut into bite-sized wedges or squares. It makes an excellent appetizer for a Korean BBQ feast, a satisfying snack, or a light meal on its own. Pair it with a cold beer, soju, or makgeolli for an authentic Korean experience, especially when the weather outside is gloomy.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a star, you can easily adapt the recipe to suit your preferences or what you have on hand:

*   **Vegetarian Pajeon**: Omit the seafood entirely and increase the amount of vegetables. Mushrooms (shiitake, oyster), zucchini, bell peppers, and kimchi are excellent additions for a meat-free version. You can even make a Kimchi Pajeon, which is another popular variant.
*   **Meat Additions**: For those who enjoy meat, thinly sliced beef (bulgogi style), pork belly, or even shredded chicken can be added alongside or instead of seafood.
*   **Different Seafood**: Don't limit yourself to the suggested seafood. Scallops, small fish fillets, or even imitation crab meat can be used. Just ensure all seafood is fresh and cut to a size that cooks quickly.
*   **Spicier Kick**: For extra heat, increase the amount of gochugaru in the dipping sauce or add some finely chopped fresh red or green chilies directly into the batter.
*   **Batter Enhancements**: Some recipes call for a small amount of cornstarch or potato starch in addition to or instead of rice flour for an even crispier texture. A dash of sugar can also help with browning.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's a celebration of simple, fresh ingredients transformed into something truly special. With these tips and a little practice, you'll be making restaurant-quality Korean seafood pancakes that will impress everyone at your table. Enjoy the journey of bringing a piece of Korea into your kitchen!
